## Feature: Branding ‚Äî Full Zed ‚Üí /nir rebrand

- **Files:**
  - `crates/zed/Cargo.toml` ‚Äî package `name = "void"`, `authors = ["Void Team"]`, `default-run = "void"`, all bundle identifiers use `dev.void.*`
  - `crates/zed/src/main.rs` ‚Äî error/launch messages say "/nir failed to launch", version string is `/nir/{version}`
  - `crates/zed/src/zed/app_menus.rs` ‚Äî Menu says `/nir`, About /nir, Hide /nir, Quit /nir, links to `niride.com`
  - `crates/zed_actions/src/lib.rs` ‚Äî doc comments reference /nir, `void_predict_onboarding` action module
  - `crates/windows_resources/src/windows_resources.rs` ‚Äî Windows app name = "Nir", "Nir Team", copyright
  - `assets/settings/initial_user_settings.json` ‚Äî header comment says `/nir settings`, links to `niride.com/docs/configuring-nir`
  - `crates/zed/src/zed.rs` ‚Äî GPU warning uses `VOID_ALLOW_EMULATED_GPU` env var, About window title "About /nir"
  - `crates/worktree/src/worktree.rs` ‚Äî doc comments reference `/void` UI
  - `crates/zed_env_vars/src/zed_env_vars.rs` ‚Äî env var docs say `/void is running in stateless mode`
  - `crates/zed_credentials_provider/src/zed_credentials_provider.rs` ‚Äî dev credential comments reference `/void`
  - `README.md` ‚Äî fully rewritten for /nir: philosophy, pillars, build instructions
  - `void-docs/NIR_BRANDING.md` ‚Äî branding guidelines document
- **What it does:** Complete fork rebrand. "Zed" ‚Üí "/nir" in all user-facing strings, menus, error messages, about dialogs, docs, and build metadata. Binary name is `void`. Bundle identifiers use `dev.void.*`. App name on Windows is "Nir".
- **Status:** KEEP

## Feature: Nir Themes (Custom Theme Pack)

- **Files:**
  - `assets/themes/nir/nir.json` ‚Äî single JSON file containing **6 theme variants**:
    1. **Nir Dark** ‚Äî amber accent (`#e8a825`), near-black backgrounds, warm muted crayon syntax
    2. **Nir Mono** ‚Äî monochrome with amber accent, muted grey syntax
    3. **Nir Abyss** ‚Äî deep teal/green dark, amber accents
    4. **Nir Forest** ‚Äî forest green palette, warm gold accent (`#c4a668`)
    5. **Nir Chalk** ‚Äî light theme, soft lavender accent (`#6050d0`)
    6. **Nir Claudious** ‚Äî warm dark, burnt-orange Claude-esque accent (`#D97757`)
- **Upstream themes pruned:** All upstream Zed themes removed except `ayu/`, `gruvbox/`, `one/`. Theme directory now contains only: `ayu`, `gruvbox`, `nir`, `one`.
- **What it does:** Replaces Zed's bundled themes with /nir-branded alternatives. Amber (#e8a825) is the primary accent color across dark themes.
- **Status:** KEEP

---

## Feature: Agent Launcher ‚Äî Full Feature

- **Files:**
  - `crates/workspace/src/agent_launcher.rs` ‚Äî `AgentLauncherButton` status-bar item; opens `AgentLauncherPage` in a split (if files open) or active pane
  - `crates/workspace/src/agent_launcher_modal.rs` ‚Äî modal variant of the launcher (legacy/alternate entry point)
  - `crates/workspace/src/agent_launcher_page.rs` ‚Äî **1,435-line full-page UI**: lists installed terminal AI agents, detects binaries via `which`/`where`, launches them into terminals via `SpawnInTerminal`, shows live running-agent preview with streaming output polling (300ms interval), config hot-reload from `terminal-agents.json`
  - `crates/workspace/src/agent_config.rs` ‚Äî `AgentConfig` struct and `load_config()`. Reads `%APPDATA%\void\terminal-agents.json` (Windows) or `~/.config/void/terminal-agents.json` (Linux/macOS). Default 8 agents: Gemini CLI, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, OpenCode, Aider, Open Interpreter, Hermes Agent, Plandex
  - `crates/void_agent_launcher/src/lib.rs` ‚Äî older/simplified `AgentLauncherButton` crate (spawns `opencode` directly; may be legacy)
  - `void-docs/AGENT_LAUNCHER.md` ‚Äî feature documentation
  - `void-docs/TERMINAL_AUTOMATION.md` ‚Äî terminal automation notes
- **What it does:**
  - Full dashboard for discovering, installing, and launching TUI AI coding agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Aider, etc.) directly inside /nir terminal panes.
  - Polls running agent terminals every 300ms and shows live streaming output in a collapsible "RUNNING AGENTS" panel at the top of the launcher page.
  - Config is a JSON file the user can edit; changes are hot-reloaded every 15s.
  - **Key integration:** Subscribes to `terminal::Event::Wakeup` to track output from agent terminals. Terminal ‚Üí Thread linkage is partially present here (uses `task.spawned_task.id` prefixed with `"agent-"` to identify agent sessions). This is the foundation for TUI agent history saving to threads.
  - **Spawner & Workspace Resilience (2026-05-24)**: Improved robustness by decoupling spawner registration from panel load to resolve race conditions, resolved borrow-checker double-lease panics on launch, and implemented home/user profile directory fallbacks for launching agents inside folderless (empty) workspaces.
- **Status:** KEEP

## Feature: Welcome Screen ‚Äî /nir Custom Design

- **Files:**
  - `crates/workspace/src/welcome.rs` ‚Äî **Fully rewritten** welcome page. Brutalist card layout. Contains:
    - `VoidLogo` component: animated blinking cursor (`‚ñä`) next to `[/] nir` in Intel One Mono, accent-colored
    - "THINK. BUILD. SHIP." tagline
    - Action rows: NEW FILE, OPEN PROJECT, CLONE REPOSITORY, SETTINGS (with keyboard shortcuts)
    - "Collaborate with Agents" card with link to agent panel (`Ctrl-Shift-/`)
    - Footer buttons: KEYMAPS, COMMAND PALETTE, ONBOARDING (accent-highlighted, links to AI onboarding)
  - Tab title returns `"/nir"` (not "Welcome")
- **What it does:** Replaces Zed's stock welcome screen with a compact, brutalist-styled card. No diagnostics/theme picker. Entry point to onboarding flow via footer button.
- **Status:** KEEP

## Feature: Status Bar Modifications

- **Files:**
  - `crates/workspace/src/status_bar.rs` ‚Äî **Custom layout**: `render_left_tools()` shows only "essential" items (BranchIndicator, ActiveBufferLanguage, CursorPosition, DiagnosticIndicator, LspButton, SearchButton). Non-essential left items are moved to overflow on the right side. The `[/]` mark used as toggle label in previous iterations is handled via the icon. `render_hideable_item()` exists but is marked `#[allow(unused)]` ‚Äî standard right-click hide behavior is defined but the current render path calls simplified versions.
  - Earlier commits: agent toggle button was added to status bar, then moved; project/agent labels were added to bottom bar.
- **What it does:** Simplified status bar layout ‚Äî essential left items only, overflow pushed right. Agent launcher button was previously in the status bar (older commits show this).
- **Status:** KEEP (modified from upstream)

## Feature: `AgentPanelTerminalFeatureFlag` ‚Äî Terminal ‚Üî Thread Integration

- **Files:**
  - `crates/feature_flags/src/flags.rs` ‚Äî `AgentPanelTerminalFeatureFlag` struct, `NAME = "agent-panel-terminal"`, `PresenceFlag` type (server-side feature flag, not staff-enabled by default)
  - `crates/agent_ui/src/agent_panel.rs` ‚Äî Multiple checks on `cx.has_flag::<AgentPanelTerminalFeatureFlag>()` controlling terminal-in-agent-panel visibility, terminal session linking to threads
  - `crates/sidebar/src/sidebar.rs` ‚Äî Terminal option gating via this flag (4+ call sites)
- **What it does:** Feature flag that controls whether terminals can appear in the agent panel and whether terminal sessions link to agent threads. **Currently server-gated** (off by default unless granted by server). This is the key integration for "TUI agent history saves to threads" (Step 4 item #1).
- **Status:** KEEP ‚Äî critical for future Agent Launcher ‚Üî Thread history feature
